title: Top 5 Insurance Mistakes That Cost New Yorkers Thousands
excerpt: From choosing the cheapest policy without checking coverage to never reviewing at renewal — these are the five most expensive insurance mistakes and exactly how to avoid them.
author: Peerless Brokerage Team
body:
After 25+ years helping Flushing, Queens, and NYC-area families and businesses with their insurance, we've seen the same costly mistakes repeat over and over. Here are the five that hurt our clients most — and how to avoid them.

## Mistake #1: Buying on Price Alone Without Checking Coverage

This is the most common and most costly mistake. Two auto policies with the same premium can have dramatically different coverage. One might include rental car reimbursement, roadside assistance, and high liability limits. The other might be bare-minimum state requirements.

The right approach: compare policies on both price AND coverage. An independent broker can show you the differences side-by-side.

## Mistake #2: Carrying State Minimum Liability Only

New York's minimum liability limits ($25,000/$50,000 for auto, for example) were set decades ago and haven't kept pace with medical costs or litigation. A serious accident today can easily generate $500,000 or more in damages.

If your policy only covers $25,000, you're personally responsible for the rest. The difference in premium between minimum coverage and adequate coverage is often surprisingly small — sometimes $150–$300 per year.

## Mistake #3: Not Reviewing Coverage When Life Changes

Insurance needs change constantly: you buy a home, start a business, hire employees, get married, have children. Most people set up their insurance once and never think about it again.

Schedule an annual review with your broker. Major life events should trigger an immediate coverage review.

## Mistake #4: Auto-Renewing Without Shopping

Insurance carriers regularly offer introductory rates to new customers. After 2–3 years, your rate may have crept up significantly while comparable coverage from competitors has stayed flat. Insurance loyalty is rarely rewarded.

Always get competitive quotes at renewal time. Even if you stay with your current carrier, knowing market rates gives you negotiating leverage.

## Mistake #5: Filing Small Claims and Losing Preferred Status

Many policyholders file small claims — $800 for a fender bender, $1,200 for a theft — without realizing that each claim stays on their record for 3–5 years and can result in surcharges that far exceed the claim amount.

Before filing any claim, calculate: How much is the claim minus my deductible? How much might my rate increase annually for the next 3 years? Often, paying out of pocket is significantly cheaper.

## The Solution: Work with an Independent Broker

An experienced independent broker helps you avoid all five of these mistakes by:
- Comparing coverage across carriers (not just price)
- Reviewing your coverage annually
- Shopping the market at every renewal
- Advising on claim decisions based on long-term cost impact

Peerless Brokerage has been serving Flushing and NYC-area clients for over 25 years. Call (718) 799-0472 for a free insurance review.
